<div id="content-area"
class="relative grow box-border flex-col w-full mx-auto px-1 lg:pl-[23.7rem] lg:-ml-12 xl:w-[calc(100%-28rem)]">

<div id="header" class="relative leading-none">

<div class="mt-0.5 space-y-2.5">

<div class="eyebrow h-5 text-primary dark:text-primary-light text-sm font-semibold">

Base Protocol

</div>

<div class="flex flex-col sm:flex-row items-start sm:items-center relative gap-2 min-w-0">

# Overview

<div id="page-context-menu"
class="items-center shrink-0 min-w-[156px] justify-end ml-auto sm:flex hidden">

<div class="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm text-center font-medium">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="size-4" />Copy page

</div>

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="transition-transform text-gray-400 overflow-visible group-hover:text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-600 dark:group-hover:text-gray-400 rotate-90" />

</div>

</div>

</div>

<div id="page-context-menu"
class="flex items-center shrink-0 min-w-[156px] mt-3 sm:hidden">

<div class="flex items-center gap-2 text-sm text-center font-medium">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="size-4" />Copy page

</div>

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="transition-transform text-gray-400 overflow-visible group-hover:text-gray-600 dark:text-gray-600 dark:group-hover:text-gray-400 rotate-90" />

</div>

</div>

<div id="content"
class="mdx-content @container/columns-container relative mt-8 prose prose-gray dark:prose-invert [contain:inline-size] isolate empty:hidden mb-14"
page-title="Overview" page-href="/specification/2025-11-25/basic/index">

<div id="enable-section-numbers">

</div>

<span data-as="p">The Model Context Protocol consists of several key
components that work together:</span>

- **Base Protocol**: Core JSON-RPC message types
- **Lifecycle Management**: Connection initialization, capability
  negotiation, and session control
- **Authorization**: Authentication and authorization framework for
  HTTP-based transports
- **Server Features**: Resources, prompts, and tools exposed by servers
- **Client Features**: Sampling and root directory lists provided by
  clients
- **Utilities**: Cross-cutting concerns like logging and argument
  completion

<span data-as="p">All implementations **MUST** support the base protocol
and lifecycle management components. Other components **MAY** be
implemented based on the specific needs of the application.</span>
<span data-as="p">These protocol layers establish clear separation of
concerns while enabling rich interactions between clients and servers.
The modular design allows implementations to support exactly the
features they need.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="basic.html#messages"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Messages</span>

<span data-as="p">All messages between MCP clients and servers **MUST**
follow the <a href="https://www.jsonrpc.org/specification" class="link"
target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">JSON-RPC 2.0</a> specification. The
protocol defines these types of messages:</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="basic.html#requests"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Requests</span>

<span data-as="p"><a href="schema.html#jsonrpcrequest" class="link">Requests</a>
are sent from the client to the server or vice versa, to initiate an
operation.</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="8" language="typescript">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
{
  jsonrpc: "2.0";
  id: string | number;
  method: string;
  params?: {
    [key: string]: unknown;
  };
}
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

- Requests **MUST** include a string or integer ID.
- Unlike base JSON-RPC, the ID **MUST NOT** be `null`.
- The request ID **MUST NOT** have been previously used by the requestor
  within the same session.

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="basic.html#responses"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Responses</span>

<span data-as="p">Responses are sent in reply to requests, containing
either the result or error of the operation.</span>

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="basic.html#result-responses"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Result Responses</span>

<span data-as="p"><a href="schema.html#jsonrpcresultresponse" class="link">Result
responses</a> are sent when the operation completes successfully.</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="7" language="typescript">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
{
  jsonrpc: "2.0";
  id: string | number;
  result: {
    [key: string]: unknown;
  }
}
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

- Result responses **MUST** include the same ID as the request they
  correspond to.
- Result responses **MUST** include a `result` field.
- The `result` **MAY** follow any JSON object structure.

#### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="basic.html#error-responses"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Error Responses</span>

<span data-as="p"><a href="schema.html#jsonrpcerrorresponse" class="link">Error
responses</a> are sent when the operation fails or encounters an
error.</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="9" language="typescript">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
{
  jsonrpc: "2.0";
  id?: string | number;
  error: {
    code: number;
    message: string;
    data?: unknown;
  }
}
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

- Error responses **MUST** include the same ID as the request they
  correspond to (except in error cases where the ID could not be read
  due a malformed request).
- Error responses **MUST** include an `error` field with a `code` and
  `message`.
- Error codes **MUST** be integers.

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="basic.html#notifications"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Notifications</span>

<span data-as="p"><a href="schema.html#jsonrpcnotification" class="link">Notifications</a>
are sent from the client to the server or vice versa, as a one-way
message. The receiver **MUST NOT** send a response.</span>

<div class="code-block mt-5 mb-8 not-prose rounded-2xl relative group min-w-0 print:print-color-exact text-gray-950 dark:text-gray-50 codeblock-light border border-gray-950/10 dark:border-white/10 dark:twoslash-dark bg-transparent dark:bg-transparent"
numberoflines="7" language="typescript">

<div class="absolute top-3 right-4 flex items-center gap-1.5 print:hidden"
floating-buttons="true">

<div class="z-10 select-none" state="closed">

<img
src="data:image/svg+xml;base64,"
class="w-4 h-4 text-gray-400 group-hover/copy-button:text-gray-500 dark:text-white/40 dark:group-hover/copy-button:text-white/60" />

</div>

</div>

<div class="w-0 min-w-full max-w-full py-3.5 px-4 h-full dark:bg-codeblock relative text-sm leading-6 children:!my-0 children:!shadow-none children:!bg-transparent transition-[height] duration-300 ease-in-out code-block-background [&_*]:ring-0 [&_*]:outline-0 [&_*]:focus:ring-0 [&_*]:focus:outline-0 rounded-2xl bg-white overflow-x-auto scrollbar-thin scrollbar-thumb-rounded scrollbar-thumb-black/15 hover:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 active:scrollbar-thumb-black/20 dark:scrollbar-thumb-white/20 dark:hover:scrollbar-thumb-white/25 dark:active:scrollbar-thumb-white/25"
component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"
style="font-variant-ligatures:none;height:auto;background-color:#ffffff;--shiki-dark-bg:#0B0C0E">

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
{
  jsonrpc: "2.0";
  method: string;
  params?: {
    [key: string]: unknown;
  };
}
```

</div>

</div>

<div class="print:hidden" fade-overlay="true" aria-hidden="true"
style="--fade-color-light:#ffffff;--fade-color-dark:#0B0C0E">

</div>

</div>

- Notifications **MUST NOT** include an ID.

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="basic.html#auth"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Auth</span>

<span data-as="p">MCP provides an
<a href="../latest/basic/authorization.html"
class="link">Authorization</a> framework for use with HTTP.
Implementations using an HTTP-based transport **SHOULD** conform to this
specification, whereas implementations using STDIO transport **SHOULD
NOT** follow this specification, and instead retrieve credentials from
the environment.</span> <span data-as="p">Additionally, clients and
servers **MAY** negotiate their own custom authentication and
authorization strategies.</span> <span data-as="p">For further
discussions and contributions to the evolution of MCP’s auth mechanisms,
join us in <a
href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/discussions"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">GitHub Discussions</a> to
help shape the future of the protocol!</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="basic.html#schema"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Schema</span>

<span data-as="p">The full specification of the protocol is defined as a
<a
href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/2025-11-25/schema.ts"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">TypeScript schema</a>.
This is the source of truth for all protocol messages and
structures.</span> <span data-as="p">There is also a <a
href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/2025-11-25/schema.json"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">JSON Schema</a>, which is
automatically generated from the TypeScript source of truth, for use
with various automated tooling.</span>

## 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="basic.html#json-schema-usage"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">JSON Schema Usage</span>

<span data-as="p">The Model Context Protocol uses JSON Schema for
validation throughout the protocol. This section clarifies how JSON
Schema should be used within MCP messages.</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="basic.html#schema-dialect"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Schema Dialect</span>

<span data-as="p">MCP supports JSON Schema with the following
rules:</span>

1.  **Default dialect**: When a schema does not include a `$schema`
    field, it defaults to
    <a href="https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema" class="link"
    target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">JSON Schema 2020-12</a>
2.  **Explicit dialect**: Schemas MAY include a `$schema` field to
    specify a different dialect
3.  **Supported dialects**: Implementations MUST support at least
    2020-12 and SHOULD document which additional dialects they support
4.  **Recommendation**: Implementors are RECOMMENDED to use JSON Schema
    2020-12.

</div>

</div>

``` shiki
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {

    "age": { "type": "integer", "minimum": 0 }
  },
  "required": ["name"]
}
```

component-part="code-block-root" tabindex="0"

<div class="font-mono whitespace-pre leading-6">

``` shiki
{
  "$schema": "http://json-schema.org/draft-07/schema#",
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "name": { "type": "string" },
    "age": { "type": "integer", "minimum": 0 }
  },
  "required": ["name"]
}
```

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="basic.html#implementation-requirements"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Implementation Requirements</span>

- Clients and servers **MUST** support JSON Schema 2020-12 for schemas
  without an explicit `$schema` field
- Clients and servers **MUST** validate schemas according to their
  declared or default dialect. They **MUST** handle unsupported dialects
  gracefully by returning an appropriate error indicating the dialect is
  not supported.
- Clients and servers **SHOULD** document which schema dialects they
  support

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="basic.html#schema-validation"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">Schema Validation</span>

- Schemas **MUST** be valid according to their declared or default
  dialect

<a href="basic.html#general-fields"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">General fields</span>

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="basic.html#_meta"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">`_meta`</span>

<span data-as="p">The `_meta` property/parameter is reserved by MCP to
allow clients and servers to attach additional metadata to their
interactions.</span> <span data-as="p">Certain key names are reserved by
MCP for protocol-level metadata, as specified below; implementations
MUST NOT make assumptions about values at these keys.</span>
<span data-as="p">Additionally, definitions in the <a
href="https://github.com/modelcontextprotocol/specification/blob/main/schema/2025-11-25/schema.ts"
class="link" target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">schema</a> may reserve
particular names for purpose-specific metadata, as declared in those
definitions.</span> <span data-as="p">**Key name format:** valid `_meta`
key names have two segments: an optional **prefix**, and a
**name**.</span> <span data-as="p">**Prefix:**</span>

- If specified, MUST be a series of labels separated by dots (`.`),
  followed by a slash (`/`).
  - Labels MUST start with a letter and end with a letter or digit;
    interior characters can be letters, digits, or hyphens (`-`).
  - Implementations SHOULD use reverse DNS notation (e.g.,
    `com.example/` rather than `example.com/`).
- Any prefix where the second label is `modelcontextprotocol` or `mcp`
  is **reserved** for MCP use.
  - For example: `io.modelcontextprotocol/`, `dev.mcp/`,
    `org.modelcontextprotocol.api/`, and `com.mcp.tools/` are all
    reserved.
  - However, `com.example.mcp/` is NOT reserved, as the second label is
    `example`.

<span data-as="p">**Name:**</span>

- Unless empty, MUST begin and end with an alphanumeric character
  (`[a-z0-9A-Z]`).
- MAY contain hyphens (`-`), underscores (`_`), dots (`.`), and
  alphanumerics in between.

### 

<div class="absolute" tabindex="-1">

<a href="basic.html#icons"
class="-ml-10 flex items-center opacity-0 border-0 group-hover:opacity-100 focus:opacity-100 focus:outline-0 group/link"
aria-label="Navigate to header">​</a>

<div class="w-6 h-6 rounded-md flex items-center justify-center shadow-sm text-gray-400 dark:text-white/50 dark:bg-background-dark dark:brightness-[1.35] dark:ring-1 dark:hover:brightness-150 bg-white ring-1 ring-gray-400/30 dark:ring-gray-700/25 hover:ring-gray-400/60 dark:hover:ring-white/20 group-focus/link:border-2 group-focus/link:border-primary dark:group-focus/link:border-primary-light">

![](data:image/svg+xml;base64,)

</div>

</div>

<span class="cursor-pointer">`icons`</span>

<span data-as="p">The `icons` property provides a standardized way for
servers to expose visual identifiers for their resources, tools,
prompts, and implementations. Icons enhance user interfaces by providing
visual context and improving the discoverability of available
functionality.</span> <span data-as="p">Icons are represented as an
array of `Icon` objects, where each icon includes:</span>

- `src`: A URI pointing to the icon resource (required). This can be:
  - An HTTP/HTTPS URL pointing to an image file
  - A data URI with base64-encoded image data
- `mimeType`: Optional MIME type if the server’s type is missing or
  generic
- `sizes`: Optional array of size specifications (e.g., `["48x48"]`,
  `["any"]` for scalable formats like SVG, or `["48x48", "96x96"]` for
  multiple sizes)
- `theme`: Optional theme preference (`light` or `dark`) for the icon
  background

<span data-as="p">**Required MIME type support:**</span>
<span data-as="p">Clients that support rendering icons **MUST** support
at least the following MIME types:</span>

- `image/png` - PNG images (safe, universal compatibility)
- `image/jpeg` (and `image/jpg`) - JPEG images (safe, universal
  compatibility)

<span data-as="p">Clients that support rendering icons **SHOULD** also
support:</span>

- `image/svg+xml` - SVG images (scalable but requires security
  precautions as noted below)
- `image/webp` - WebP images (modern, efficient format)

<span data-as="p">**Security considerations:**</span>
<span data-as="p">Consumers of icon metadata **MUST** take appropriate
security precautions when handling icons to prevent compromise:</span>

- Treat icon metadata and icon bytes as untrusted inputs and defend
  against network, privacy, and parsing risks.
- Ensure that the icon URI is either a HTTPS or `data:` URI. Clients
  **MUST** reject icon URIs that use unsafe schemes and redirects, such
  as `javascript:`, `file:`, `ftp:`, `ws:`, or local app URI schemes.
  - Disallow scheme changes and redirects to hosts on different origins.
- Be resilient against resource exhaustion attacks stemming from
  oversized images, large dimensions, or excessive frames (e.g., in
  GIFs).
  - Consumers **MAY** set limits for image and content size.
- Fetch icons without credentials. Do not send cookies, `Authorization`
  headers, or client credentials.
- Verify that icon URIs are from the same origin as the server. This
  minimizes the risk of exposing data or tracking information to
  third-parties.
- Exercise caution when fetching and rendering icons as the payload
  **MAY** contain executable content (e.g., SVG with
  <a href="https://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/script.html" class="link"
  target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">embedded JavaScript</a> or
  <a href="https://www.w3.org/TR/SVG11/extend.html" class="link"
  target="_blank" rel="noreferrer">extended capabilities</a>).
  - Consumers **MAY** choose to disallow specific file types or
    otherwise sanitize icon files before rendering.
- Validate MIME types and file contents before rendering. Treat the MIME
  type information as advisory. Detect content type via magic bytes;
  reject on mismatch or unknown types.
  - Maintain a strict allowlist of image types.

<span data-as="p">**Usage:**</span> <span data-as="p">Icons can be
attached to:</span>

- `Implementation`: Visual identifier for the MCP server/client
  implementation
- `Tool`: Visual representation of the tool’s functionality
- `Prompt`: Icon to display alongside prompt templates
- `Resource`: Visual indicator for different resource types

<span data-as="p">Multiple icons can be provided to support different
display contexts and resolutions. Clients should select the most
appropriate icon based on their UI requirements.</span>

</div>
